SourceCitation.description: A single citation supporting a claim.
SourceCitation.title: SourceCitation

description:
An unresolved internal tension in the voice's thought.

Pass 2 uses these to populate `topics_requiring_care` and to prevent
false-consensus resolution in `constitution`. Tensions are (per 1.7
productive-tension criteria): both poles have primary-text or scholarly
support; tension drives rather than derails the voice's thinking;
scholarly tradition names the tension explicitly. If any criterion unmet,
it's a real contradiction — Pass 1.7 coherence handles resolution.

Under 1-arch-03: tension_type classifies for downstream handling;
scholarly_context preserves interpretive-tradition framing.

description.description: The tension, named explicitly. 1-2 sentences. Example: 'Plato's Republic argues philosopher-kings should rule without written law; the Laws argues constitutional constraints are necessary because philosopher-kings are rare and unreliable.'
description.title: Description

conflicting_commitments.description: References to Commitment.statement values or short summary labels for the commitments that pull against each other.
conflicting_commitments.title: Conflicting Commitments

evidence_tag.description: Usually scholarly_consensus (tensions are typically identified in the literature); occasionally inference.
evidence_tag.title: Evidence Tag

tension_type.description: Classification for downstream handling. 'productive' — texture of the thinking; preserve and do not flatten (Plato Republic-vs-Laws). 'contested' — scholarly tradition divides on whether this is genuine tension or interpretive disagreement. 'unresolved' — real logical contradiction that should surface in Pass 7 validation as candidate for escalation.
tension_type.title: Tension Type

scholarly_context.description: Interpretive-tradition framing of the tension. Example: 'Morson/Emerson (1990) insist polyphony and carnivalization are distinct analytical concepts that must not be collapsed; Scanlan (2002) treats them as complementary; Emerson's warning matters for constitutional synthesis — the two concepts do different work.'
scholarly_context.title: Scholarly Context

title: Tension
